raw_cv_text:
Ghost Gary
rmingham Birmingham, UK
Bachelor of Science, Computer Science Sep. 2023 – (Expected) June 2026
Technical Skills
Programming languages: C, Haskell, Java, JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, SQL, HTML, CSS
Frameworks and libraries: React, Node.js, Express, Spring Boot, JavaFX
Tools: Git, GitHub Actions, Docker, Postman, Maven
Experience
Freelance Developer Aug 2024 – Sep 2024
• Collaborated with a client to design and implement an automated email workflow using the SendGrid API,
streamlining email campaigns, reminders, and responses to key user interactions
• Built a custom referral system with subscription-based incentives, enabling users to easily generate and share
personalized referral links to boost engagement
• Achieved a 13% increase in user engagement within two weeks, driven by a surge in new sign-ups and reactivation
of previously inactive users
Technology & Data Insight Programme July 2023 – Aug 2023
Lloyds Banking Group
• Engineered predictive models to assess customer overdraft behavior, utilising transactional data with pandas and
scikit-learn to improve the efficiency of the data processing pipeline.
• Successfully predicted repeat overdraft customers with an accuracy of ˜72% using logistic regression, enhancing the
decision-making process for financial risk management.
• Created insightful data visualisations using matplotlib and presented findings to a panel of senior stakeholders
Projects
Educache | Spring Boot, JPA, Hibernate, React, PostgreSQL, Docker, OpenAI API Aug 2024 – Present
∗ Created a full-stack student management system, implementing key features for managing student profiles,
academic records and performance tracking
∗ Integrated OpenAI’s API to automate feedback generation, allowing tutors to quickly produce detailed reports,
cutting manual input and review time by 57%
∗ Dockerised the entire application, ensuring smooth deployment across different environments
Advanced News Classifier | Java, Deeplearning4J, ND4J Oct 2023 – Dec 2023
∗ Developed an advanced machine learning system to classify news articles into distinct categories, leveraging GloVe
embeddings to capture semantic relationships between words
∗ Employed pre-trained GloVe embeddings to represent words as dense vectors, enabling the model to understand
context and meaning beyond individual words
∗ Built a robust data pre-processing pipeline, incorporating essential steps like text cleaning, lemmatization, and
stop word filtering, ensuring the data was optimally prepared for model training
∗ Trained the classifier using document-level embeddings, using ND4J to optimize array manipulation, significantly
improving training efficiency
Volunteering
Tutor & Academic Mentor Feb 2022 – Present
∗ Deliver personalised tutoring sessions in computer science, mathematics, biology, and chemistry, using Educache
to efficiently manage student profiles and track academic progress
∗ Design and implement personalised learning plans, monitor academic progress, and provide regular, detailed
feedback reports to address specific learning challenges
name: Ghost Gary
